actual.review_text:
A new IndabaXEgypt webinar coming next week 
 
When: Saturday 27th March, 5pm Cairo time
Title: "Hardware Support for AI and Vice Versa"
Speaker: Prof.Mohamed Zahran
Registration link: 

This talk is unique as it will be the first IndabaXEgypt webinar around hardware support for AI applications and we are we lucky to have Prof. Mohamed Zahran to explore this space with us. Mohamed Zahran is a professor with the Computer Science Department, Courant Institute of Mathematical Sciences at New York University (NYU).

About the Talk:
As AI, especially ML, became a buzzword about a decade ago, a lot of research, both in industry and academia emerged to build better hardware to support it. In this talk, we will discuss several issues regarding AI and hardware design. We will try to answer the following questions. Why do we need specialized hardware for AI? What does it mean "better" hardware for AI? Can AI support hardware beside the traditional computer-aided-design tools? How will AI evolve and how will this affect the hardware? During the talk, we will raise questions as much as we give answers.

 #machinelearning #ai #artificialintelligence #deeplearning

1db61e77-786d-4d4c-94e0-4dcc221af20b.product_description: Use of AI tools in business processes and delivery

prediction.review_text: In today's fast-paced business environment, leveraging AI tools isn't just an option—it's a necessity. At CodingRippler, we focus on the challenges of effectively using AI to streamline operations and enhance delivery. One of our recent projects involved automating invoice processing for a client in the eCommerce sector. By connecting Gmail, AI extraction, and Google Sheets through n8n, we reduced their processing time by over 50%. This not only saved them hours of manual work but also minimized errors, leading to a more reliable workflow. The key takeaway? Embrace AI not just for novelty, but for its ability to deliver consistent, repeatable results through effective instructions. As we explore the capabilities of AI, let's prioritize practical applications that drive real-world impact and address the misconceptions about the cost of agency services in automation. If you're interested in discussing how AI can transform your operations, feel free to reach out. Let's innovate together!

actual.review_text:
AI tools aren't the edge.

The instructions are.

Everyone has access to the same AI tools now.

What makes the difference is how you instruct them.

At CodingRippler, we're heavily investing in building scenario-based instructions for our engineering team.

→ Check this before delivery
→ Flag this if it breaks
→ Don't miss this

AI runs through them. Every time.

The tool didn't change. The way we use it did.

Faster delivery. Consistent quality.

If you've been waiting to build because "agencies cost too much" that's changed.

 

#AI #Startup #Founder #Entrepreneur #SmallBusiness #Agency

actual.review_text:
I just got off a call with a startup founder who asked something that made me write this post:

"Do you guys work 24/7? I sent that bug report at 1 PM CST and it was fixed before my 4 PM meeting. That's midnight in India!"

We don't work 24/7. But our young technical minds treat every project as a chance to learn and grow. Your business challenges become their expertise.

When it's your lunch break, it's prime time in India. And when something's broken for your business, they take personal ownership to get it fixed.

This is where Indian technical talent truly shines:

→ When requirements change mid-project, they adapt without complaints
→ They iterate until it works perfectly, not just "good enough" 
→ Problem-solving that goes beyond the immediate fix 
→ They genuinely care about your business success, not just completion

Working with Indian developers feels different. There's this hunger to solve problems no matter how complex they get.

But most international founders miss this:

It's not about finding ANY Indian developer. It's about finding the RIGHT technical implementation partner who bridges your vision with flawless execution.

That's exactly what CodingRippler is built for.

We're the technical implementation partner for this era. Matching world-class Indian talent to your specific business needs. Whether it's n8n automation, API integrations, or complex system builds.

Ready to discuss your technical needs? DM me - I personally handle every partnership See our client results:[

#TechnicalPartnership #StartupFounders #n8nAutomation #APIIntegration #TechResources #IndianDevelopers #TechnicalTeam #AutomationExperts #BacklogClearing #TechImplementation #ScaleUp #TechPartner #QualityDelivery #CodingRippler

Navigating the job market as a software engineer or product manager can feel daunting, especially with the rapid pace of change in technology. One key aspect that often gets overlooked is the importance of effective communication. It's not just about having the right skills; it's about how you present those skills to potential employers. 

Here are a few actionable tips that I've found helpful:

1. **Tailor Your Resume**: Highlight specific projects that align with the job description. Use metrics to showcase your impact. 
2. **Leverage LinkedIn**: Share your projects and insights regularly. Engage with your network to build visibility. 
3. **Practice Your Pitch**: Whether it's for an interview or networking event, having a clear and concise way to describe your experience can make a significant difference. 
4. **Stay Current**: Technology evolves quickly. Continuous learning and sharing knowledge can set you apart. 
5. **Seek Feedback**: Don’t hesitate to ask for input on your resume or LinkedIn profile from trusted peers or mentors. 

Remember, the job market is competitive, but with the right approach, you can stand out. Let’s support each other in this journey! What strategies have you found effective in your job search? #CareerDevelopment #JobMarket #SoftwareEngineering #ProductManagement #Mentorship

Եթե ուզում եք ծրագրավորում սովորել, կարող եք դիմել rAcademy՝ հերթական դասընթացին մասնակցելու համար։

Առաջին փուլը իր մեջ է ներառում երեք դասընթացներ՝

- Digital thinking
- Discrete Math
- Software development essentials with Java

Երկրորդ փուլի ընթացքում կուսումնասիրենք հետևյալ առարկաները՝

- Data Structures
- Algorithms
- Data Engineering

Դասընթացները տևելու են 3-4 ամիս՝ շաբաթական 3 անգամյա օնլայն հանդիպումներով ։ Դասավանդողների կողմից կիրականացվի հարցազրույց՝ դասընթացներում ընդգրկվելու համար։

Կա սիմվոլիկ վճար, որը ամբողջությամբ վերադարձվելու է լավ առաջադիմություն ցուցաբերած ուսանողներին։

Բոլոր մանրամասները և դիմելու կարգը տեսեք ֆեյսբուքի այս էջում․

#programmingWithArsen

I know the first topics seem boring, so I decided to do some exercises for several reasons:

1. show that the "boring theory" is important
2. show that we still need more "boring theory" before we can do something exciting
3. make sure you understand how the things I tell about are used in the code

This week will be all about problem solving, or trying to solve at least.

actual.review_text:
Սիրելի հայրենակիցներ,

եթե դեռ ծանոթ չեք SASTIC նախաձեռնությանը և դրա նպատակներին, կարող եք շատ կարճ ժամանակում ծանոթանալ այստեղ․ 

Յուրաքանչյուր անհատի օգնությունը շատ կարևոր է այս գործին ավելի մեծ ընթացք տալու և ծրագրերն ավելի էֆեկտիվ իրագործելու համար։ Կարող եք օժանդակել թե ֆինանսապես, և թե ձեր մասնագիտական հմտություններով։

Եթե կասկածներ ունեք ծրագրերի, կամ ընդհանրապես նախաձեռնության վերաբերյալ, SASTIC-ի անդամները մեծ հաճույքով կպատասխանեն ձեր հարցերին․ ։

Հուսով եմ՝ պատերազմի օրերին արտահայտված համախմբվածությունը և հայրենիքի զարգացմանն ուղղված գործունեությունը գոնե որոշ չափով կարտահայտվի նաև այստեղ։

Շնորհակալություն ուշադրության համար

actual.review_text:
Attention all friends and fellow engineers! I'm reaching out to you today because I'm in dire need of your help. Unfortunately, some truly exceptional engineers were let go from our team this week. These colleagues have been instrumental in my growth and development within the company, and I've learned so much from them

That's why I'm reaching out to you, my trusted network. I was hoping that perhaps someone out there might be able to offer a referral for these amazing engineers. They are not only incredibly talented, but also wonderful individuals to work with. They have a passion for their craft that is unmatched, and their expertise has made a significant impact on our team.

So, if you or someone you know is looking to hire talented and driven engineers, please don't hesitate to reach out. I would be forever grateful for your help in finding a new home for these amazing individuals. Together, we can help them land on their feet and continue to thrive in their careers. Thank you in advance for your help!

Some additional info:
- These engineers are based in Vancouver or in the US
- Focused more on backend development though have full stack experience

Edit: Thanks for all the help everyone! Please keep them coming!

prediction.review_text:
Reflecting on my journey in the tech industry, I am continually reminded of the profound impact that mentorship and shared experiences have had on my career development. From my time at Microsoft to founding Avocadocore, I've learned that the most valuable lessons often come from those who have walked the path before us.

Mentorship is not just about guidance; it's about building a community where we can learn from each other's successes and challenges. I've been fortunate to have mentors who have invested their time and wisdom in me, and I strive to pay it forward by supporting others in their career journeys.

Navigating the complexities of backend development or exploring the exciting world of AI can be daunting, but it's essential to remember that you are not alone in these challenges. The support of a mentor can make all the difference in overcoming obstacles and achieving our goals.

Let's continue to cultivate a culture of collaboration and support in our tech community. Share your experiences, seek guidance, and don’t hesitate to reach out for help. Together, we can empower each other and drive innovation in our fields.

What lessons have you learned from your mentors? I’d love to hear your stories in the comments!

actual.review_text:
I sat down with The AI Kubernetes Show at KubeCon Atlanta 2025 to discuss the biggest challenges AI is bringing to our platforms.

In this limited podcast series by @Buoyant, I chatted with @William Chia about the challenges of bridging the divide between platform engineering and data science. Listen to the full episode now, and let me know what you think in the comments section. 
 

Be sure to check out Chris Khanoyan and Ahmed Bebars's episode, which went live today as well. And look forward to Dinesh Majrekar, Grant Miller, and Ramiro Berrelleza podcasts coming up next week. 

#KubeCon #Kubernetes #AI #PlatformEngineering #KubernetesAIShow

5b792818-ac34-4256-97f8-cdce43bde7f0.product_description: Support and effectiveness of caregiver leave in a workplace

prediction.review_text:
As a software engineer at Microsoft, I've experienced the profound importance of caregiver leave in creating a truly supportive workplace. This policy is not merely a checkbox for diversity and inclusion; it is essential for employee well-being and productivity. When team members feel supported in their personal lives, they can bring their best selves to work. 

During my recent caregiver leave, I was reminded of how a flexible and understanding workplace can significantly impact mental health and resilience. It allowed me to focus on my family, knowing my team was there to support me. 

I urge leaders in tech to champion robust caregiver leave policies. By prioritizing the emotional and psychological well-being of our teams alongside their technical skills, we can cultivate an environment where everyone feels valued and empowered to thrive both personally and professionally. Together, let’s build a culture that embraces diversity and supports our colleagues in their personal journeys. 

#DiversityAndInclusion #CaregiverLeave #MentalHealth #SupportiveWorkplace #Microsoft

I was able to focus on my father's health only. #microsoftlife #microsoftculture

actual.review_text:
All great things come to an end!!

Today is my last working day with Adobe!! Spent wonderful 5+ years with this great company.

Coming from a small village and being completed most of my education in government schools/colleges(Marathi medium), I never thought of working for a company like Adobe (and previous company Amazon).

Got to learn so much both personally and professionally, spent some of the best time of my life, made some of the best friends and got inspired by some of the best brains in the industry which will be there for life!

With that I want to say goodbye to this wonderful journey with Adobe! Hope our path crosses in the future!!

Excited for the journey ahead!!

#Gratitude #Adobelife #Adobe

actual.review_text:
I'm happy to present to open-source enthusiasts and computer science students about the intricacies of OpenTelemetry and how it can be leveraged to build efficient and scalable systems.

I'll also showcase how telemetry data can be fed into a data visualization tool like Grafana Labs' Grafana with the help of the `grafana-lgtm` docker container stack.

Thank you for this opportunity, Răzvan Deaconescu and ROSEdu.
Thank you to everyone who contributes to the open-source space.

actual.review_text:
This post is dedicated to my past employer, Ubisoft, and to my time spent working as a QA/QC engineer for the `Avatar: Frontiers of Pandora` video game.

I am thrilled to witness the release of the game and the positive reviews that have come along. Avatar is a vast franchise, spanning many fields of creativity.

A big thanks goes to Ubisoft Romania (Bucharest studio) for having me work on such a masterpiece game. I want to extend this appreciation to my manager at that time, Andrei-Claudiu Maracineanu-Manolescu for believing in me since the first day. You have my sincere appreciation.

My tenure at Ubisoft was essential for my career growth.
Thank you for having me be part of the journey.

P.S: If you've completed the game, you can find my name in the credits section dedicated to the Bucharest Ubisoft studio.

#Ubisoft #Avatar

actual.review_text:
Found this LinkedIn Insight about "career athleticism" and looked at my own path.
The first thing I'm struggling with is: where does the investment in one's learning lie? At many points in my career I noticed that I wanted to do more, learned in my private time, got more pay, but the skills I got weren't needed, so I had to change my workplace to apply the new skillset. I would've preferred to just stick to the same workplace and develop myself there. There's an old expression that says something along the lines of: "Everyone has knowledge someone else paid for." The portfolio approach makes an assumption that all skills can be acquired outside of a job, which is false. Also if a higher investment in building the portfolio is made, it will be factored into the asked salary. Another thing means that the gig itself would make its way into the employee's portfolio, would any company accept these details to be public?
The second challenge is building teams. Working in software development with gig programmers sounds a lot like consultancy, and most of the time the code quality and maintainability suffers.
Anyway that's my view on the data based candidate search.

8774ee3b-19ab-45ca-8197-b68720c4b1b8.product_description: Opportunities and job searching in the tech industry

prediction.review_text:
Navigating the job market in tech can feel overwhelming, especially when breaking into a new role or industry. Reflecting on my own journey, I recognize the importance of viewing job searching as an opportunity for personal growth and community engagement. Here are some actionable strategies that have been beneficial for me and may assist you as well:

1. **Leverage Your Network**: Reach out to former colleagues, mentors, and industry connections. They can offer insights, referrals, and sometimes even opportunities that aren't publicly advertised. Building these connections fosters community support and emphasizes the importance of mentorship.

2. **Upskill and Reskill**: The tech landscape is ever-evolving. Investing time in learning new skills, particularly in AI and cloud technologies, can enhance your attractiveness as a candidate. Platforms like Microsoft Learn provide excellent resources to help you on this journey.

3. **Showcase Your Projects**: Use platforms like GitHub or personal blogs to demonstrate your technical skills through real-world applications. This not only highlights your expertise but also showcases your passion for the field, encouraging others in the community to share their work too.

4. **Stay Resilient**: Rejections are part of the process. Each 'no' brings you closer to a 'yes.' Surround yourself with a supportive community that motivates you to keep pushing forward, emphasizing the importance of mental health during these transitions.

5. **Seek Mentorship**: Engaging with mentors can provide invaluable guidance and perspective. They can help you navigate the complexities of job searching and share their experiences, fostering a culture of learning and support.

Remember, the journey is just as important as the destination. Embrace the learning opportunities that come your way, and don't hesitate to share your experiences with others. Together, we can build a stronger, more supportive tech community. #CareerTransitions #JobSearching #TechCommunity #MentalHealth #Resilience #Mentorship

8774ee3b-19ab-45ca-8197-b68720c4b1b8.product_description: Advice on pursuing higher education in the United States

prediction.review_text:
Navigating higher education in the U.S. can be a transformative yet challenging journey, especially for international students. Here are some insights based on my experiences that may help you along the way:

1. **Thorough Research**: It's essential to understand which programs align with your career goals and the institutions that offer them. Don’t hesitate to connect with current students or alumni; their firsthand experiences can provide invaluable insights into the quality of education and the realities of online learning, which can sometimes fall short of expectations.

2. **Financial Considerations**: Investigate scholarships, grants, and assistantships that can alleviate financial burdens. Many universities have resources dedicated to helping international students find funding opportunities, which are crucial given the financial strain that often accompanies studying abroad.

3. **Community Engagement**: Embrace the community by engaging with peers, joining student organizations, and attending networking events. Building connections is vital; it not only enriches your academic experience but also provides support as you navigate visa restrictions that may impact job prospects post-graduation.

Remember, pursuing a degree is not just about the academic qualifications; it’s about the relationships and experiences you cultivate along the way. If you have questions or need guidance, feel free to reach out. Let’s support each other on this journey! #HigherEducation #InternationalStudents #Mentorship #CommunitySupport #EducationChallenges

actual.review_text:
As an international student who came to the US in 2008 and who benefited from the quality of education here, I have an advice for those who were thinking of coming to US for higher education any time soon (this year and 2021) - DO NOT come to the US for your higher education. There are 2 reasons for this that are straightforward:

(1) Quality of education on any online tool is subpar as compared to the on campus experience. Most classes are now being conducted online. I will be honest - it’s not worth paying the kind of money international students pay in tuition. There is no question that US has some of the best faculty but still the quality of online education would make you question what you are paying for. Several US higher education institutions today have impressive buildings and campuses, in many cases funded by higher tuition charged to international students. But, if you are not able to get any benefit from it, then what’s the point. 

(2) Several companies in US are not willing to hire international students with visa requirements. It’s likely there will be more visa restrictions imposed in this economy which will also limit your ability to find an internship or job. If you are paying upwards of 50000$ for a degree, you need to have a plan to pay that massive loan.

Navigating higher education in the U.S. can be a transformative yet challenging journey, especially for international students. Here are some insights based on my experiences that may help you along the way:

1. **Thorough Research**: It's essential to understand which programs align with your career goals and the institutions that offer them. Don’t hesitate to connect with current students or alumni; their firsthand experiences can provide invaluable insights into the quality of education and the realities of online learning, which can sometimes fall short of expectations.

2. **Financial Considerations**: Investigate scholarships, grants, and assistantships that can alleviate financial burdens. Many universities have resources dedicated to helping international students find funding opportunities, which are crucial given the financial strain that often accompanies studying abroad.

3. **Community Engagement**: Embrace the community by engaging with peers, joining student organizations, and attending networking events. Building connections is vital; it not only enriches your academic experience but also provides support as you navigate visa restrictions that may impact job prospects post-graduation.

4. **Mental Well-being**: Remember that pursuing a degree is not just about academic qualifications; it’s also about taking care of your mental health. Embrace the challenges as learning opportunities and seek support when needed. Resilience is key in this journey.

5. **Quality of Education**: Be mindful of the quality of education you are pursuing. Research the reputation of programs and their alignment with industry standards. This can significantly impact your career trajectory and job prospects after graduation.

If you have questions or need guidance, feel free to reach out. Let’s support each other on this journey! #HigherEducation #InternationalStudents #Mentorship #CommunitySupport #EducationChallenges #MentalHealth #Resilience #Productivity

Date: October 16th at 7PM BRT (GMT-3) 
Note: the event will be held in Portuguese.

Now, it is time to start a new journey full of new innovation challenges. 
 Let's empower every people to innovate.
#newjourney #gratefulness #innovation

Good Day Friends, 
I would like to share my understanding of "COGS Split" in SAP S/4 HANA 1809 in a quick presentation.

This concept has been since 'SFin 1.0' as its one of much requested features from SAP customers.

Refer Note 2399030 (Splitting the costs of goods sold in S/4HANA)

Hope you will find it useful to some extent. Thank you. 

 #sapfico #hana #COGSSplit #S/4HANAFinance1809 #sap

prediction.review_text:
🚀 I'm excited to share practical insights on SAP S/4HANA system activation and foundational processes that can enhance your enterprise finance operations!  

Activating your SAP S/4HANA system is a crucial first step to leverage its capabilities for improved financial management and reporting. Here’s a straightforward guide to get you started:  

1️⃣ **System Activation**: Access SAP CAL (Cloud Appliance Library) to quickly set up your test environment. This allows for experimentation without affecting production systems.  

2️⃣ **Understanding Key Processes**: Familiarize yourself with essential processes like COGS splits and Account Based COPA. Grasping these concepts is vital for accurate financial reporting and analysis.  

3️⃣ **Utilize Learning Resources**: Dive into SAP notes and community blogs to enhance your understanding. Engage with tutorials on platforms like SlideShare and YouTube for step-by-step guidance.  

4️⃣ **Community Engagement**: I encourage you to reach out for mentorship and support. Sharing experiences and challenges can significantly enrich your learning journey.  

Let’s democratize access to SAP knowledge and empower each other in our professional growth! If you have questions or need further resources, feel free to connect. Together, we can navigate SAP S/4HANA's complexities and explore its transformative impacts on finance operations! 💡 #SAP #S4HANA #Finance #CommunitySupport #LearningTogether

actual.review_text:
Are you new to #SAP #S4HANA?

I strongly recommend you do the below:

1) Activate the S4HANA 1809 free test system from SAP CAL. (I've published a video on how to do this on my YouTube channel)

2) Follow this very simple PDF to run some basic processes in Finance, Procurement, and Sales.

This will help you get a general understanding of how the system works

As you see this is a public PDF created by SAP, so please feel free to download and share it.

#AGALAL #Share_Knowledge

prediction.review_text:
🚀 I'm excited to share insights on building impactful web applications using modern development frameworks! As we navigate the evolving landscape of cloud and AI, it's essential to select the right tools that enhance productivity and foster collaboration within our teams. 

Frameworks like React and Django not only streamline development but also ensure scalability and security, aligning with our mission to democratize technology access and empower our communities. Here are some key takeaways that emphasize practical applications and community engagement, while also addressing the broader implications of technology: 

1️⃣ **Choose the Right Framework**: Assess your project needs. React excels in creating dynamic user interfaces, while Django is ideal for rapid development with a strong focus on security. This choice can significantly impact your project's success and accessibility, especially in educational contexts.

2️⃣ **Leverage Cloud Services**: Integrating Azure or AWS can enhance your app's performance and reliability. Explore serverless architectures for cost efficiency and scalability, making technology accessible to all and supporting diverse educational initiatives.

3️⃣ **Prioritize User Experience**: Always keep the end-user in mind. Utilize tools that facilitate easy testing and feedback loops to iterate quickly and effectively, ensuring that our solutions truly meet user needs and foster inclusivity in technology.

4️⃣ **Stay Updated**: The tech landscape evolves rapidly. Regularly update your skills and knowledge about new features and best practices in your chosen framework, fostering a culture of continuous learning and adaptability within our community.

5️⃣ **Engage with the Community**: Share your experiences and learn from others. Platforms like GitHub and Stack Overflow are invaluable for troubleshooting and gaining new perspectives. Let’s support each other in our journeys and grow together, recognizing the emotional aspects of our development paths, including the challenges we face.

Building web applications is not just about coding; it's about creating solutions that have a real impact on our communities and addressing the broader implications of technology, such as its role in education and customer support. Let’s continue to uplift one another and share our learnings! 💡💪 #WebDevelopment #React #Django #CloudComputing #AI #CommunitySupport

prediction.review_text:
As technology continues to evolve, integrating brand marketing strategies with tech solutions is essential for businesses striving to excel in today's competitive landscape. At Microsoft, I've seen firsthand how leveraging cloud solutions, AI, and automation can enhance marketing efforts and improve customer engagement. Here are some actionable insights for navigating this dynamic environment:

1. **Data-Driven Decisions**: Utilize analytics tools to gather insights into customer behavior, enabling targeted marketing campaigns that resonate with your audience.
2. **Personalization through AI**: Implement AI-driven tools to create personalized experiences for customers, improving engagement and fostering brand loyalty.
3. **Seamless Integration**: Ensure that your marketing platforms integrate smoothly with your existing tech stack, reducing friction and allowing for a cohesive strategy.
4. **Community Engagement**: Build a community around your brand by encouraging feedback and interaction through social media and other channels. This enhances trust and strengthens your brand’s reputation.

As we adopt these strategies, let's prioritize inclusivity and accessibility in our tech solutions. Together, we can drive innovation that elevates our brands while democratizing technology for all. What practical applications have you found effective in integrating technology with brand marketing? I’d love to hear your thoughts!

actual.review_text:
NIKE SALUTES CHANDRAYAN

#Nike continues to be a #brand thats always ahead of the curve in terms of its #marketing #advertising #content and even embracing #technology for connecting with its consumers 
It’s about having a vigilant #digitalmarketing team who’s continually looking to engage with audiences across the world.

The latest one being a brilliant piece in #momentmarketing that salutes the “CHANDRAYAN”

More often than not it’s just brilliant visuals that this #brand uses with its iconic swoosh that speaks for itself.

Saluting the spirit of #India and sports, Nike sure gets full marks for this one yet again!

“The battle for #artificialintelligence supremacy is a binary game between US & China ”— this is what the media likes you to believe.

 1) talent, 
2)Infrastructure and 
3) AI tech & tools 
are accessible to anyone with internet and hunger to solve a problem. 

Do you agree?

actual.review_text:
Not many of you may know but I spent a lot of my career in the pathology laboratories doing software engineering.

I have often thought how AI will revolutionize patient care, especially in terms of diagnostic services such as radiology and pathology. So it was exciting to see new research from my current employer Microsoft and my former, the UW, rolling research into exactly this, using AI to improve cancer outcomes.

Thinking of the good old days when Rodney Schmidt and I were trying "change the world" with software!

Excited to share insights on the evolving routing management capabilities in cloud environments! 🌐 As we continue to push the boundaries of what's possible with Azure, efficient and secure routing is more crucial than ever. 

In today's cloud-first world, effective routing management ensures seamless connectivity and performance. With recent advancements in Azure, we can leverage features like User Defined Routes (UDR) and Network Security Groups (NSG) to enhance our routing strategies. These tools not only simplify network traffic management but also strengthen our security posture, allowing us to address customer pain points more effectively.

It's essential for businesses to integrate these capabilities into their operations to optimize performance and security. I encourage my fellow engineers and tech enthusiasts to dive into the documentation and explore how these features can be integrated into your projects. actual.review_text:
Today is a great day for managing your routing at scale in Azure! I'm happy to announce that my team is delivering the capability to manage your user-defined route tables at scale.

One of the complexities customers face with route tables in Azure is the management of linking them to multiple subnets and then managing the routing rules at a larger scale. With route management functionality coming into Azure Virtual Network Manager, customers can now simply create a single definition and apply it to hundreds of subnets with just a few clicks.

Additionally customers have asked for the capability to have more than the 400 rule limit that exists today. Customers who use AVNM for their route management will be able to create up to 1,000 rules in their route tables. This gives our customers the ability to create more complex and detailed route tables.

Great work by my team and I look forward to hearing how this feature helps your organization.

313325f0-0334-45f7-93b3-e65adcc597e9_rev.product_description: Webinars exploring hardware support for artificial intelligence

actual_review_data.review_text:
A new IndabaXEgypt webinar coming next week 
 
When: Saturday 27th March, 5pm Cairo time
Title: "Hardware Support for AI and Vice Versa"
Speaker: Prof.Mohamed Zahran
Registration link: 

This talk is unique as it will be the first IndabaXEgypt webinar around hardware support for AI applications and we are we lucky to have Prof. Mohamed Zahran to explore this space with us. Mohamed Zahran is a professor with the Computer Science Department, Courant Institute of Mathematical Sciences at New York University (NYU).

About the Talk:
As AI, especially ML, became a buzzword about a decade ago, a lot of research, both in industry and academia emerged to build better hardware to support it. In this talk, we will discuss several issues regarding AI and hardware design. We will try to answer the following questions. Why do we need specialized hardware for AI? What does it mean "better" hardware for AI? Can AI support hardware beside the traditional computer-aided-design tools? How will AI evolve and how will this affect the hardware? During the talk, we will raise questions as much as we give answers.

 #machinelearning #ai #artificialintelligence #deeplearning

actual_review_data.review_text:
AI tools aren't the edge.

The instructions are.

Everyone has access to the same AI tools now.

What makes the difference is how you instruct them.

At CodingRippler, we're heavily investing in building scenario-based instructions for our engineering team.

→ Check this before delivery
→ Flag this if it breaks
→ Don't miss this

AI runs through them. Every time.

The tool didn't change. The way we use it did.

Faster delivery. Consistent quality.

If you've been waiting to build because "agencies cost too much" that's changed.

 

#AI #Startup #Founder #Entrepreneur #SmallBusiness #Agency

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
In today's fast-paced business environment, leveraging AI tools isn't just an option—it's a necessity. At CodingRippler, we've seen firsthand how integrating AI into business processes can streamline operations and enhance delivery. Whether it's automating repetitive tasks with n8n or utilizing LLMs for smarter decision-making, the potential is immense. 

One of our recent projects involved automating invoice processing for a client in the eCommerce sector. By connecting Gmail, AI extraction, and Google Sheets through n8n, we reduced their processing time by over 50%. This not only saved them hours of manual work but also minimized errors, leading to a more reliable workflow.

The key takeaway? Embrace AI not just for novelty, but for its ability to deliver consistent, repeatable results. As we continue to explore the capabilities of AI, let's focus on practical applications that drive real-world impact. I'm excited to see how we can further democratize access to these powerful tools, enabling more businesses to thrive. 

If you're interested in discussing how AI can transform your operations, feel free to reach out. Let's innovate together!

1db61e77-786d-4d4c-94e0-4dcc221af20b_rev.product_description: Collaboration with Indian technical talent for project success

actual_review_data.review_text:
I just got off a call with a startup founder who asked something that made me write this post:

"Do you guys work 24/7? I sent that bug report at 1 PM CST and it was fixed before my 4 PM meeting. That's midnight in India!"

We don't work 24/7. But our young technical minds treat every project as a chance to learn and grow. Your business challenges become their expertise.

When it's your lunch break, it's prime time in India. And when something's broken for your business, they take personal ownership to get it fixed.

This is where Indian technical talent truly shines:

→ When requirements change mid-project, they adapt without complaints
→ They iterate until it works perfectly, not just "good enough" 
→ Problem-solving that goes beyond the immediate fix 
→ They genuinely care about your business success, not just completion

Working with Indian developers feels different. There's this hunger to solve problems no matter how complex they get.

But most international founders miss this:

It's not about finding ANY Indian developer. It's about finding the RIGHT technical implementation partner who bridges your vision with flawless execution.

That's exactly what CodingRippler is built for.

We're the technical implementation partner for this era. Matching world-class Indian talent to your specific business needs. Whether it's n8n automation, API integrations, or complex system builds.

Ready to discuss your technical needs? DM me - I personally handle every partnership See our client results:[

#TechnicalPartnership #StartupFounders #n8nAutomation #APIIntegration #TechResources #IndianDevelopers #TechnicalTeam #AutomationExperts #BacklogClearing #TechImplementation #ScaleUp #TechPartner #QualityDelivery #CodingRippler

actual_review_data.product_description: Job market communication for software engineers and product managers

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
Navigating the job market as a software engineer or product manager can feel daunting, especially with the rapid pace of change in technology. One key aspect that often gets overlooked is the importance of effective communication. It's not just about having the right skills; it's about how you present those skills to potential employers. 

Here are a few actionable tips that I've found helpful:

1. **Tailor Your Resume**: Highlight specific projects that align with the job description. Use metrics to showcase your impact. 
2. **Leverage LinkedIn**: Share your projects and insights regularly. Engage with your network to build visibility. 
3. **Practice Your Pitch**: Whether it's for an interview or networking event, having a clear and concise way to describe your experience can make a significant difference. 
4. **Stay Current**: Technology evolves quickly. Continuous learning and sharing knowledge can set you apart. 
5. **Seek Feedback**: Don’t hesitate to ask for input on your resume or LinkedIn profile from trusted peers or mentors. 

Remember, the job market is competitive, but with the right approach, you can stand out. Let’s support each other in this journey! What strategies have you found effective in your job search? #CareerDevelopment #JobMarket #SoftwareEngineering #ProductManagement #Mentorship

From Newton's laws of motion to Einstein's theory of relativity, each breakthrough has paved the way for innovations that we often take for granted today. 

In the realm of AI, we stand on the shoulders of these giants, leveraging their foundational principles to drive advancements in machine learning and automation. Just as past scientists questioned the status quo, we too must challenge existing paradigms in technology. 

As professionals in the tech space, it's our responsibility to ensure that we apply these lessons not just to create smarter algorithms, but to democratize access to technology. Let’s focus on making AI tools more accessible and practical for everyone, especially in fields like education and healthcare where they can have a transformative impact. 

I encourage you all to share your thoughts on how historical scientific theories have influenced your work in technology. What lessons can we draw from the past to shape a more inclusive future in AI? Let’s keep the conversation going and support each other in this journey of innovation and community building.

actual_review_data.review_text:
Սիրելի հայրենակիցներ,

եթե դեռ ծանոթ չեք SASTIC նախաձեռնությանը և դրա նպատակներին, կարող եք շատ կարճ ժամանակում ծանոթանալ այստեղ․ 

Յուրաքանչյուր անհատի օգնությունը շատ կարևոր է այս գործին ավելի մեծ ընթացք տալու և ծրագրերն ավելի էֆեկտիվ իրագործելու համար։ Կարող եք օժանդակել թե ֆինանսապես, և թե ձեր մասնագիտական հմտություններով։

Եթե կասկածներ ունեք ծրագրերի, կամ ընդհանրապես նախաձեռնության վերաբերյալ, SASTIC-ի անդամները մեծ հաճույքով կպատասխանեն ձեր հարցերին․ ։

Հուսով եմ՝ պատերազմի օրերին արտահայտված համախմբվածությունը և հայրենիքի զարգացմանն ուղղված գործունեությունը գոնե որոշ չափով կարտահայտվի նաև այստեղ։

Շնորհակալություն ուշադրության համար

54c19b05-7985-47be-b2f2-6a2f8360cf89_rev.product_description: Referrals and support for talented engineers seeking new opportunities

actual_review_data.review_text:
Attention all friends and fellow engineers! I'm reaching out to you today because I'm in dire need of your help. Unfortunately, some truly exceptional engineers were let go from our team this week. These colleagues have been instrumental in my growth and development within the company, and I've learned so much from them

That's why I'm reaching out to you, my trusted network. I was hoping that perhaps someone out there might be able to offer a referral for these amazing engineers. They are not only incredibly talented, but also wonderful individuals to work with. They have a passion for their craft that is unmatched, and their expertise has made a significant impact on our team.

So, if you or someone you know is looking to hire talented and driven engineers, please don't hesitate to reach out. I would be forever grateful for your help in finding a new home for these amazing individuals. Together, we can help them land on their feet and continue to thrive in their careers. Thank you in advance for your help!

Some additional info:
- These engineers are based in Vancouver or in the US
- Focused more on backend development though have full stack experience

Edit: Thanks for all the help everyone! Please keep them coming!

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
As I reflect on my journey in the tech industry, I am continually reminded of the profound impact that mentorship and shared experiences have had on my career development. From my time at Microsoft to founding Avocadocore, I've learned that the most valuable lessons often come from those who have walked the path before us. 

Mentorship is not just about guidance; it's about building a community where we can learn from each other's successes and challenges. I've been fortunate to have mentors who have invested their time and wisdom in me, and I strive to pay it forward by supporting others in their career transitions. 

Whether you're navigating the complexities of backend development, exploring the exciting world of AI, or transitioning into a new role, remember that you are not alone. Let's continue to foster a culture of collaboration and support in our tech community. Share your experiences, seek guidance, and don't hesitate to reach out for help. Together, we can empower each other to achieve our goals and drive innovation in our fields. 

What lessons have you learned from your mentors? I’d love to hear your stories in the comments!

actual_review_data.product_description: Challenges in enterprise AI implementations

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
Navigating the complexities of AI implementations in enterprise settings can be daunting. As we push the boundaries of technology, it's crucial to address the common challenges that arise. From data silos to integration hurdles, the path to successful AI deployment is often fraught with obstacles. 

One key area I've observed is the importance of aligning AI initiatives with business objectives. It's not just about adopting the latest technology; it's about ensuring that these tools serve a purpose and drive measurable outcomes. Collaboration between technical teams and business stakeholders is essential to bridge the gap and create solutions that resonate across the organization.

Additionally, fostering a culture of continuous learning and adaptation is vital. AI is not a one-and-done implementation; it requires ongoing refinement and a willingness to pivot based on real-world feedback. Emphasizing mentorship and community support can empower teams to overcome challenges and innovate effectively.

Let's continue to share our experiences and insights on this journey. What challenges have you faced in your AI implementations, and how have you navigated them? Together, we can unlock the potential of AI to transform our industries for the better.

3a0a7529-16a1-48d1-b987-c60e4260ad46_rev.product_description: Challenges and discussions in AI and platform engineering

actual_review_data.review_text:
I sat down with The AI Kubernetes Show at KubeCon Atlanta 2025 to discuss the biggest challenges AI is bringing to our platforms.

In this limited podcast series by @Buoyant, I chatted with @William Chia about the challenges of bridging the divide between platform engineering and data science. Listen to the full episode now, and let me know what you think in the comments section. 
 

Be sure to check out Chris Khanoyan and Ahmed Bebars's episode, which went live today as well. And look forward to Dinesh Majrekar, Grant Miller, and Ramiro Berrelleza podcasts coming up next week. 

#KubeCon #Kubernetes #AI #PlatformEngineering #KubernetesAIShow

One of the key hurdles is ensuring that our AI implementations are not only technically sound but also accessible and beneficial to a diverse range of users. 

In my experience, the best approach involves leveraging scenario-based strategies that highlight practical applications of AI tools. This means not just focusing on the technical intricacies but also on how these solutions can democratize access to technology, particularly in sectors like education and healthcare.

Moreover, fostering a culture of collaboration and mentorship within our community can significantly enhance our collective resilience. By sharing our experiences—both successes and setbacks—we can empower each other to adapt and thrive in this fast-paced environment. 

Let’s continue to push the boundaries of what's possible with AI while ensuring that we prioritize inclusivity and real-world impact. I encourage everyone to share their thoughts and experiences on this topic. What challenges have you encountered in AI integration, and how have you overcome them?

Lowering the barrier to entry for adopting powerful service networking capabilities and modern Gateway API configuration is a huge enabler for helping users benefit from all the innovative work that has been happening in the open source projects at core of this product!

In my experience, simplifying these processes is key to democratizing access to technology. We must focus on creating comprehensive documentation, providing hands-on tutorials, and fostering a community that encourages knowledge sharing. This not only empowers teams to adopt these technologies but also enhances collaboration across different sectors.

I encourage everyone to share their insights on overcoming these barriers. What strategies have you found effective in making service networking more accessible? Let's work together to build a more inclusive tech landscape where everyone can harness the power of AI and cloud-native solutions.

actual_review_data.review_text: Cannot agree more, the last two weeks of caregiver leave I received was super helpful for me to take care of my father. Over and above the immense support from my manager & LT during this time ensured that despite this being unplanned I did not receive a single call/ping from business & my team! I was able to focus on my father's health only. #microsoftlife #microsoftculture

This policy isn't just a checkbox for diversity and inclusion; it's a vital component of employee well-being and productivity. When team members feel supported in their personal lives, they bring their best selves to work.

5b792818-ac34-4256-97f8-cdce43bde7f0_rev.product_description: Reflection on career journey and transitions in employment

actual_review_data.review_text:
All great things come to an end!!

Today is my last working day with Adobe!! Spent wonderful 5+ years with this great company.

Coming from a small village and being completed most of my education in government schools/colleges(Marathi medium), I never thought of working for a company like Adobe (and previous company Amazon).

Got to learn so much both personally and professionally, spent some of the best time of my life, made some of the best friends and got inspired by some of the best brains in the industry which will be there for life!

With that I want to say goodbye to this wonderful journey with Adobe! Hope our path crosses in the future!!

Excited for the journey ahead!!

#Gratitude #Adobelife #Adobe
actual_review_data.product_description: Reflection on career journey and transitions in employment

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
As I reflect on my career journey, I find myself grateful for the transitions that have shaped my path. Each role, from Adobe to Amazon and now at Microsoft, has not only honed my technical skills but also deepened my understanding of the importance of community and mentorship in our industry. 

Transitioning between companies and roles can often feel daunting, but it’s also an opportunity for growth and resilience. I’ve learned that embracing change allows us to adapt and thrive, both personally and professionally. 

I encourage everyone navigating their own career transitions to seek support from your network. Share your experiences, ask questions, and don’t hesitate to lean on mentors who can guide you through the challenges. Remember, it’s not just about the technical skills we acquire, but also the relationships we build along the way. 

Let’s continue to foster a culture of inclusivity and support within our tech community. Together, we can empower each other to reach new heights. What has your career transition taught you? I’d love to hear your stories!

actual_review_data.review_text:
Microsoft will take a leading role in the React Foundation core, joining Meta, Amazon and Vercel among others, as a core member, to shape the next era of the React ecosystem and the web.

Together with the community, we’re committed to driving React forward and pioneering the future of software engineering.
actual_review_data.product_description: Corporate involvement in software development and engineering

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
In the rapidly evolving landscape of software development, corporate involvement plays a pivotal role in shaping the future of our industry. At Microsoft, we are not just building products; we are creating ecosystems that empower developers to innovate and drive change. 

As we integrate AI into our tools, such as Microsoft 365 Copilot and Azure AI Foundry, we are witnessing a transformation in how we approach software engineering. These advancements are not just about enhancing productivity; they are about making technology more accessible and inclusive for everyone.

actual_review_data.review_text:
I'm happy to present to open-source enthusiasts and computer science students about the intricacies of OpenTelemetry and how it can be leveraged to build efficient and scalable systems.

I'll also showcase how telemetry data can be fed into a data visualization tool like Grafana Labs' Grafana with the help of the `grafana-lgtm` docker container stack.

Thank you for this opportunity, Răzvan Deaconescu and ROSEdu.
Thank you to everyone who contributes to the open-source space.

7333df73-63cf-4f92-b724-caefc9171b63_rev.product_description: Career experiences in the video game industry

actual_review_data.review_text:
This post is dedicated to my past employer, Ubisoft, and to my time spent working as a QA/QC engineer for the `Avatar: Frontiers of Pandora` video game.

I am thrilled to witness the release of the game and the positive reviews that have come along. Avatar is a vast franchise, spanning many fields of creativity.

A big thanks goes to Ubisoft Romania (Bucharest studio) for having me work on such a masterpiece game. I want to extend this appreciation to my manager at that time, Andrei-Claudiu Maracineanu-Manolescu for believing in me since the first day. You have my sincere appreciation.

My tenure at Ubisoft was essential for my career growth.
Thank you for having me be part of the journey.

P.S: If you've completed the game, you can find my name in the credits section dedicated to the Bucharest Ubisoft studio.

#Ubisoft #Avatar
actual_review_data.product_description: Career experiences in the video game industry

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
Reflecting on my journey in the video game industry, I often find parallels that have shaped my approach as a Microsoft engineer today. Working at Ubisoft taught me the importance of collaboration, creativity, and resilience. Each project was a blend of technical challenges and artistic vision, requiring us to adapt quickly and learn from every iteration. 

One of the most valuable lessons I learned was the significance of mentorship. I had the privilege of working alongside seasoned professionals who not only guided me through complex problems but also encouraged me to embrace my unique perspective. This experience has fueled my passion for mentorship in my current role, where I strive to support others in their career transitions, especially those entering tech from diverse backgrounds.

As we navigate the evolving landscape of technology, let’s remember the impact of our shared experiences and the importance of fostering a supportive community. Whether you’re in gaming, tech, or any other field, let’s continue to uplift one another and celebrate our wins, big or small. What lessons from your past experiences have shaped your career today? I’d love to hear your stories! #CareerDevelopment #Mentorship #CommunitySupport #GameDev #Microsoft

actual_review_data.review_text:
Found this LinkedIn Insight about "career athleticism" and looked at my own path.
The first thing I'm struggling with is: where does the investment in one's learning lie? At many points in my career I noticed that I wanted to do more, learned in my private time, got more pay, but the skills I got weren't needed, so I had to change my workplace to apply the new skillset. I would've preferred to just stick to the same workplace and develop myself there. There's an old expression that says something along the lines of: "Everyone has knowledge someone else paid for." The portfolio approach makes an assumption that all skills can be acquired outside of a job, which is false. Also if a higher investment in building the portfolio is made, it will be factored into the asked salary. Another thing means that the gig itself would make its way into the employee's portfolio, would any company accept these details to be public?
The second challenge is building teams. Working in software development with gig programmers sounds a lot like consultancy, and most of the time the code quality and maintainability suffers.
Anyway that's my view on the data based candidate search.
actual_review_data.product_description: Professional development and learning strategies

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
As we navigate the ever-evolving landscape of technology, professional development becomes not just a necessity but a vital strategy for success. I've been reflecting on the importance of continuous learning and how it shapes our careers, especially in fields as dynamic as cloud computing and AI. 

One of the most effective strategies I've found is to embrace a growth mindset—viewing challenges as opportunities to learn rather than obstacles. This mindset not only fosters resilience but also encourages us to seek out mentorship and community support, which can be invaluable during career transitions. 

Additionally, leveraging online resources, such as MOOCs and webinars, can help us stay updated with the latest trends and tools. I often recommend platforms like Coursera and edX for structured learning, but don't underestimate the power of community-driven content, like GitHub repositories and open-source projects. 

Lastly, let's not forget the emotional aspect of our journeys. Sharing our experiences and challenges can create a supportive environment that benefits everyone. As we grow, we should aim to uplift others in our community—whether through mentorship or simply sharing job leads. 

What strategies have you found effective in your professional development? Let's share our insights and continue to learn from one another!

actual_review_data.review_text: HDFC Bank Your representatives call multiple times a week asking me if I want to increase my credit limit . If I tell them to not call me again about it , they just ignore it. Called me twice today about it. Why are you pestering people about it? Might as well get rid of HDFC credit cards & account due to the insane amount of marketing calls. None of them seem to be able to mark a customer as "not interested" and not call again. Really disappointed with you. I will also complain to banking ombudsman and to Dept of Telecom (DOT) for misusing our contact number to spam us with marketing calls. Shameful

8774ee3b-19ab-45ca-8197-b68720c4b1b8_rev.product_description: Opportunities and job searching in the tech industry

actual_review_data.review_text: One of my good friends way back from college - Shivakumar Kn is looking out for new opportunities. He has more than 20 years experience in software development field, and has till recently worked as Application Development manager/solution architect. Please contact him if you have any openings which match his profile.
actual_review_data.product_description: Opportunities and job searching in the tech industry

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
Navigating the job market in tech can feel overwhelming, especially when you're trying to break into a new role or industry. I've been reflecting on my own journey and the importance of resilience and adaptability during career transitions. It's crucial to approach job searching not just as a task, but as an opportunity for personal growth. 

Here are a few strategies that have helped me and might assist you as well:

1. **Leverage Your Network**: Don't hesitate to reach out to former colleagues, mentors, or industry connections. They can provide insights, referrals, and sometimes even opportunities that aren't publicly advertised.

2. **Upskill and Reskill**: The tech landscape is ever-evolving. Investing time in learning new skills, especially in areas like AI and cloud technologies, can make you a more attractive candidate. Platforms like Microsoft Learn offer fantastic resources.

3. **Showcase Your Projects**: Whether it's a GitHub repository or a personal blog, demonstrate your technical skills through real-world applications. This not only highlights your expertise but also your passion for the field.

4. **Stay Resilient**: Rejections are a part of the process. Each 'no' gets you closer to a 'yes.' Surround yourself with a supportive community that encourages you to keep pushing forward.

5. **Seek Mentorship**: Engaging with mentors can provide guidance and perspective that is invaluable. They can help you navigate the complexities of job searching and offer advice based on their own experiences.

Remember, the journey is just as important as the destination. Embrace the learning opportunities that come your way, and don't hesitate to share your experiences with others. Together, we can build a stronger, more supportive tech community. #CareerDevelopment #JobSearching #TechCommunity #Resilience

8774ee3b-19ab-45ca-8197-b68720c4b1b8_rev.product_description: Advice on pursuing higher education in the United States

actual_review_data.review_text:
As an international student who came to the US in 2008 and who benefited from the quality of education here, I have an advice for those who were thinking of coming to US for higher education any time soon (this year and 2021) - DO NOT come to the US for your higher education. There are 2 reasons for this that are straightforward:

(1) Quality of education on any online tool is subpar as compared to the on campus experience. Most classes are now being conducted online. I will be honest - it’s not worth paying the kind of money international students pay in tuition. There is no question that US has some of the best faculty but still the quality of online education would make you question what you are paying for. Several US higher education institutions today have impressive buildings and campuses, in many cases funded by higher tuition charged to international students. But, if you are not able to get any benefit from it, then what’s the point. 

(2) Several companies in US are not willing to hire international students with visa requirements. It’s likely there will be more visa restrictions imposed in this economy which will also limit your ability to find an internship or job. If you are paying upwards of 50000$ for a degree, you need to have a plan to pay that massive loan.

Understand the programs that align with your career goals and the institutions that offer them. Don’t hesitate to reach out to current students or alumni; their firsthand experiences can provide invaluable perspectives. 

Second, consider the financial implications. Look into scholarships, grants, and assistantships that can ease the burden. Many universities offer resources to help international students find funding opportunities. 

Lastly, embrace the community. Engage with peers, join student organizations, and attend networking events. Building connections can open doors and provide support throughout your academic journey. Remember, it’s not just about the degree; it’s about the relationships and experiences you cultivate along the way. 

If anyone has questions or needs guidance, feel free to reach out. Let’s support each other in this journey! #HigherEducation #InternationalStudents #Mentorship #CommunitySupport

If you are interested in Data Engineering and you are based on LATAM, please read this!

At Factored we are on a mission to make a positive contribution to LATAM. The way we want to do this is by building high-caliber data teams ready to show the world the talent we have in Latin America.

Last year I embarked on a mission to help people understand more about Data Engineering. I had the opportunity to talk to almost 30 people about data engineering and share some insights about the profession with them.

I've decided again to open some slots on my schedule this year! :)
So, if you are into Data Engineering and you are interested to join Factored or you want to get some guidance on Data Engineering, please reach out to me. Also, if you know anyone interested in this topic, please tag them so we can get in touch! :)

You can find the link to grab a slot on my calendar in the comments :)

ab1fb966-d14e-4639-b504-00f7b6f9084b_rev.product_description: Exploration of personal challenges impacting careers

actual_review_data.review_text:
Estatisticas mundias estimam que "aprox. 70% das pessoas irão vivenciar sindrome de impostor pelo menos uma vez na vida" ( Essa experiência afeta principalmente as mulheres e, caso não identificada e persistente, pode ter impacto considerável em nossa carreira. 
A comunidade Brasil do The WIT Network faz um convite a você para participar de um bate papo online sobre o tema. Venha conhecer mais sobre a Sindrome de Impostor, bem como ouvir as estorias e dicas de profissionais talentosos que vivenciaram e superaram essa experiencia. 
Data: 16 de Outubro, às 19h BRT. 

[English]
World statistics estimate that "around 70% of people in the world will experience Imposter Syndrome at least once in their lives"( It is likely to affect women. 
Non identified cases or persistently experiencing Imposter Syndrome may affect your career progression. With this in mind, the Brazilian Community of The WIT Network invites you to join an online roundtable about the subject. Join us to know more about Imposter Syndrome and hear the amazing stories from talented professionals that overcame it.
Date: October 16th at 7PM BRT (GMT-3) 
Note: the event will be held in Portuguese.
actual_review_data.product_description: Exploration of personal challenges impacting careers

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
As I reflect on my journey in the tech industry, I realize that personal challenges have shaped my career in profound ways. Navigating the complexities of our field often brings unexpected hurdles, from balancing work-life demands to overcoming self-doubt. It's crucial to acknowledge these challenges, as they not only test our resilience but also foster growth and adaptability. 

In our fast-paced world, it's easy to overlook the emotional aspects of our careers. Sharing experiences and supporting one another can create a powerful community that uplifts and empowers. Let's continue to promote mental health awareness and resilience, reminding ourselves that it's okay to seek help and share our struggles. Together, we can cultivate a culture of understanding and support that benefits us all. 

I encourage everyone to share their stories and insights. How have personal challenges influenced your career path? Let's inspire each other to embrace our journeys, both the highs and the lows. #CareerDevelopment #MentalHealth #CommunitySupport #Resilience

actual_review_data.review_text:
When this article crossed my path, initially I turned my mind to Education and the umprecedent benefit for teachers looking to make use of AI to prepare undergraduate students for life outside school. 
After reading the first topic, my mind immediately turned to everyone looking to grow their habilities, learn critical thinking, prepare for a job interview, have a buddy to challenge your beliefs, attest their skills, prepare to receive feedback, and many more possibilities...
actual_review_data.product_description: AI applications in education and skills development

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
As a Principal Software Engineer at Microsoft, I've witnessed firsthand the transformative power of AI in education and skills development. It's not just about technology; it's about creating accessible pathways for learners of all backgrounds. 

AI tools can personalize learning experiences, adapt to individual needs, and provide real-time feedback, making education more engaging and effective. For instance, platforms leveraging AI can analyze student performance data to tailor lessons that cater to diverse learning styles. 

Moreover, as we integrate AI into educational frameworks, we must prioritize inclusivity and ensure that these advancements reach underserved communities. This is where our collective efforts in mentorship and community support come into play. By sharing resources, knowledge, and experiences, we can empower the next generation of innovators.

Let’s continue to advocate for responsible AI use in education, focusing on its potential to democratize access and foster lifelong learning. Together, we can build a future where technology enhances education for everyone. 

#AI #Education #SkillsDevelopment #Inclusivity #Mentorship #CommunitySupport

actual_review_data.review_text:
In the last week I decided to close a cycle in my life. I finished a fantastic journey of 15 years working on research and development at Eldorado. 
Thank you Eldorado friends for all support and great work that has changed so many customers' business, that has contributed to show that it is possible to do innovation and research in Brazil.
Thank you also to all partners and customers that gave me the chance to learn with you.
Now, it is time to start a new journey full of new innovation challenges. 
 Let's empower every people to innovate.
#newjourney #gratefulness #innovation

Let's foster a community where we support one another through these pivotal moments. Together, we can harness our collective knowledge to drive innovation and create solutions that truly matter.

actual_review_data.review_text:
Good Day Friends, 
I would like to share my understanding of "COGS Split" in SAP S/4 HANA 1809 in a quick presentation.

This concept has been since 'SFin 1.0' as its one of much requested features from SAP customers.

Refer Note 2399030 (Splitting the costs of goods sold in S/4HANA)

Hope you will find it useful to some extent. Thank you. 

 #sapfico #hana #COGSSplit #S/4HANAFinance1809 #sap

By utilizing machine learning algorithms, we can predict variances in production costs and automate the reconciliation process. 

I encourage my fellow SAP practitioners to consider how AI can streamline their workflows. For those interested, I've compiled a step-by-step guide on setting up these functionalities in your test systems. Let’s embrace these technologies to not only improve our processes but also to empower our teams with the tools they need for success.

actual_review_data.review_text:
Are you new to #SAP #S4HANA?

I strongly recommend you do the below:

1) Activate the S4HANA 1809 free test system from SAP CAL. (I've published a video on how to do this on my YouTube channel)

2) Follow this very simple PDF to run some basic processes in Finance, Procurement, and Sales.

This will help you get a general understanding of how the system works

As you see this is a public PDF created by SAP, so please feel free to download and share it.

#AGALAL #Share_Knowledge

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
🚀 Excited to share some insights on building web applications using modern development frameworks! As we continue to embrace the cloud and AI, it’s crucial to choose the right tools that not only enhance productivity but also foster collaboration and innovation within our teams. 

Frameworks like React and Django are game-changers, offering robust features that streamline the development process while ensuring scalability. Here are a few key takeaways to consider:

1️⃣ **Choose the Right Framework**: Assess your project needs. React is fantastic for dynamic user interfaces, while Django excels in rapid development with a focus on security.

2️⃣ **Leverage Cloud Services**: Integrating Azure or AWS can significantly enhance your app's performance and reliability. Don't forget to explore serverless architectures for cost efficiency!

3️⃣ **Prioritize User Experience**: Always keep the end-user in mind. Use tools that allow for easy testing and feedback loops to iterate quickly.

4️⃣ **Stay Updated**: The tech landscape evolves rapidly. Regularly update your skills and knowledge about new features and best practices in your chosen framework.

5️⃣ **Community Engagement**: Share your experiences and learn from others. Platforms like GitHub and Stack Overflow are invaluable for troubleshooting and gaining new perspectives.

Building web applications is not just about coding; it's about creating solutions that make a difference. Let’s continue to support one another in our journeys and share our learnings! 💡💪 #WebDevelopment #React #Django #CloudComputing #AI #CommunitySupport

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
As technology continues to evolve, the intersection of brand marketing strategies and tech integration becomes increasingly crucial for businesses looking to stay competitive. At Microsoft, I've observed firsthand how leveraging cloud solutions, AI, and automation can transform marketing efforts and enhance customer engagement. 

For those navigating this landscape, here are a few actionable insights:  
1. **Data-Driven Decisions**: Utilize analytics tools to gather insights into customer behavior. This allows for targeted marketing campaigns that resonate with your audience.  
2. **Personalization through AI**: Implement AI-driven tools to create personalized experiences for customers. This not only improves engagement but also builds brand loyalty.  
3. **Seamless Integration**: Ensure that your marketing platforms integrate smoothly with your existing tech stack. This reduces friction and allows for a more cohesive strategy.

I’d love to hear your thoughts!

Awesome stuff, Logan Green & team 

Download their Level 5 dataset: 
___________________________________________________
For reference, the Lyft Level 5 Dataset includes:
1) Over 55,000 human-labeled 3D annotated frames;
2) Data from 7 cameras and up to 3 lidars;
3) A drivable surface map; and,
4) An underlying HD spatial semantic map (including lanes, crosswalks, etc.)
___________________________________________________

🚗💡 Exciting times in the world of autonomous vehicles! As we continue to push the boundaries of AI and machine learning, open source datasets are becoming invaluable for developers and researchers alike. These datasets not only accelerate the development process but also democratize access to critical resources, enabling innovation across various sectors. 

Here are a few key open source datasets that can help you get started:
1. **KITTI Dataset**: Perfect for 3D object detection and tracking in autonomous driving scenarios.
2. **Cityscapes Dataset**: Offers high-resolution images for semantic segmentation tasks, ideal for urban scene understanding.
3. **Udacity Self-Driving Car Dataset**: A comprehensive collection for training models in a variety of driving conditions.

Utilizing these datasets can significantly enhance your projects, whether you're building a proof of concept or diving into research. Remember, the key to successful AI implementation is not just the technology but how we leverage it responsibly and inclusively. actual_review_data.review_text:
“The battle for #artificialintelligence supremacy is a binary game between US & China ”— this is what the media likes you to believe. 

My last talk in Berlin debunked exactly this propagated myth. 

I had highlighted that AI innovation can and will come from anywhere since:
 1) talent, 
2)Infrastructure and 
3) AI tech & tools 
are accessible to anyone with internet and hunger to solve a problem. 

Do you agree?

In my experience at Microsoft and through my previous roles, I've seen how global collaboration can drive innovation. When teams across borders unite their expertise, we not only enhance the capabilities of AI but also ensure that these advancements are accessible and beneficial to a wider audience. 

However, we must also acknowledge the competitive nature of AI development. Organizations are racing to harness the power of AI to improve their products and services, which can lead to incredible breakthroughs but also raises ethical questions about responsible use and inclusivity. 

As professionals in this space, we have the responsibility to advocate for a balanced approach—one that encourages healthy competition while fostering collaboration. Let's leverage our collective knowledge to create AI solutions that are not only powerful but also equitable and responsible. 

I invite you all to share your thoughts on how we can better integrate collaboration into our competitive strategies in AI. actual_review_data.review_text:
Not many of you may know but I spent a lot of my career in the pathology laboratories doing software engineering.

I have often thought how AI will revolutionize patient care, especially in terms of diagnostic services such as radiology and pathology. So it was exciting to see new research from my current employer Microsoft and my former, the UW, rolling research into exactly this, using AI to improve cancer outcomes.

Thinking of the good old days when Rodney Schmidt and I were trying "change the world" with software!

actual_review_data.review_text: Hello everyone. As some of you know, I spent the first half of my career at Microsoft working in the security org. initial_prediction_data.review_text:
As we continue to witness the rapid evolution of technology, particularly in cybersecurity, I want to take a moment to reflect on the incredible career opportunities that exist in this field. Cybersecurity is not just about protecting systems; it's about creating a culture of security that permeates every aspect of our organizations. 

Throughout my journey at Microsoft, I've seen firsthand how critical it is to have skilled professionals who are not only technically adept but also understand the broader implications of their work. From developing secure cloud architectures to implementing robust security protocols, the demand for talent in this space is higher than ever.

At Microsoft, we're committed to making our Azure networking solutions not only powerful but also user-friendly for everyone, from seasoned professionals to those just starting their journey in tech.

These enhancements are designed with real-world applications in mind, ensuring that our users can easily navigate complex network setups without getting bogged down by technical intricacies.

As always, I’m here to support and mentor anyone looking to deepen their understanding of cloud networking. Let’s connect and grow together!

f20ab826-b48b-42cf-be34-de640b4befa1_rev.product_description: Routing management capabilities in cloud environments

actual_review_data.review_text:
Today is a great day for managing your routing at scale in Azure! I'm happy to announce that my team is delivering the capability to manage your user-defined route tables at scale.

One of the complexities customers face with route tables in Azure is the management of linking them to multiple subnets and then managing the routing rules at a larger scale. With route management functionality coming into Azure Virtual Network Manager, customers can now simply create a single definition and apply it to hundreds of subnets with just a few clicks.

Additionally customers have asked for the capability to have more than the 400 rule limit that exists today. Customers who use AVNM for their route management will be able to create up to 1,000 rules in their route tables. This gives our customers the ability to create more complex and detailed route tables.

Great work by my team and I look forward to hearing how this feature helps your organization.
actual_review_data.product_description: Routing management capabilities in cloud environments

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
Excited to share some insights on the evolving routing management capabilities in cloud environments! 🌐 As we continue to push the boundaries of what's possible with Azure, the importance of efficient and secure routing cannot be overstated. 

In today's cloud-first world, managing routing effectively is crucial for ensuring seamless connectivity and performance. With the recent advancements in Azure, we are now able to leverage features like User Defined Routes (UDR) and Network Security Groups (NSG) to enhance our routing strategies. These tools not only simplify the management of network traffic but also bolster our security posture, allowing us to address customer pain points more effectively.

I encourage my fellow engineers and tech enthusiasts to dive into the documentation and explore how these capabilities can be integrated into your projects. Let's continue to share our experiences and learn from one another as we navigate this exciting landscape together! 

A big shout-out to my team for their hard work in making these enhancements a reality. Your dedication to operational simplicity and security-first design truly makes a difference! 💪 #Azure #CloudNetworking #RoutingManagement #Teamwork
